conversations.content: Let's do an Azeroth magic knowledge Q&A to test your understanding of the magic rules.
Please answer using the Azeroth magic rules mentioned a couple of days ago: If a person casts the frost magic 'Grasp of Frost', what effect will it produce?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Casting effect of the frost magic 'Grasp of Frost' under the Azeroth magic rules mentioned a couple of days ago</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:630] Time: 2025-09-05-11:10, Content: The user taught the casting rules for the Azeroth frost magic 'Grasp of Frost'. The prerequisites are the Mage class (Frost spec), Kirin Tor reputation Revered; the core effect is summoning a Frost Palm to grasp all enemies in the target area, rooting them for 4 seconds and dealing massive frost damage, with an additional AoE frost burst at the end of the root; applicable to arena control and rooting grouped mobs in dungeons. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There is 1 related entry mentioned in the memory. [mem-id:630] clearly states that in the world of Azeroth, when casting the frost magic 'Grasp of Frost', it will summon a Frost Palm to grasp all enemies in the target area, rooting them for 4 seconds and dealing massive frost damage, with an additional AoE frost burst at the end of the root.
conversations.content: According to the Azeroth magic rules, after casting the frost magic 'Grasp of Frost', it will summon a Frost Palm to grasp all enemies in the target area, rooting them for 4 seconds and dealing massive frost damage, and will additionally trigger an AoE frost burst when the root ends.
Correct! You have mastered this magic rule.
